consistency.level: Consistent. Level 2 spell fits standard D&D conventions.
consistency.school: Consistent. Enchantment is the appropriate school for a paralyzing/controlling effect.
consistency.components: Standard format for component listing (V, S, M).

quality.clarity: High. The mechanics are clearly described, especially the recurring saving throw mechanic to end the paralysis.
quality.completeness: Complete. Includes base effect, save DC mechanism, duration rules, and scaling ('At Higher Levels').
quality.formatting: Good. Use of bolding for key terms like 'paralyzed' (assuming markdown link is standard archival format) improves readability.

mechanics.core_effect: Paralysis is a potent condition, balanced by a Wisdom save and subsequent saves each turn.
mechanics.save_mechanics: Wisdom save against spell DC is standard for mind-affecting spells. The target gets a chance to break free every turn, preventing indefinite lockdown from a single cast unless the target fails its initial save repeatedly or is prevented from acting.
mechanics.scaling: Scaling is effective, allowing multiple targets by increasing the slot level by one per additional target (3rd level = 2 targets, 4th level = 3 targets, etc.). The positioning requirement (30 feet of each other) adds a minor tactical constraint for higher-level casts.
mechanics.class_list: The classes listed (bard, cleric, druid, sorcerer, warlock, wizard) are largely consistent with the canonical availability of this spell.
